title: SPARC - Chronic implantation of gastrointestinal and vagus nerve electrodes in the ferret
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2tihc","text":"This protocol is used to surgically implant gastrointestinal planar electrodes and vagus nerve cuff electrodes for chronic electrophysiological recording and stimulaiton in the ferret. Electrode lead wiring is tunneled subcutaneously to a connector embedded in a headcap affixed to the cranium.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e3bd9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fukgj","text":"Funding: This protocol was developed with funding from the NIH Common Fund’s Stimulating Peripheral Activity to Relieve Conditions (SPARC) program (Award U18TR002205). To learn more about the SPARC program, visit https://sparc.science.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"41atd","text":"1) procedures should be conducted under sterile conditions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fi07q","text":"2) animal should be monitored for stability of respiration, heart rate, blood pressure, and temperature","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Induce anesthesia with 20mg/kg ketamine (i.m.).
Make incisions in skin on the abdomenal midline (~4 cm) and dorsal neck (~3 cm).
Use a trocar to tunnel under skin from the dorsal neck, along the back, and to the left side to exit at the abdominal incision.
Advance electrode leads and gastric catheter through tunnel with trocar and then remove trocar leaving the leads under the skin.
Place the animal on its back.

Open abdominal cavity using a 3-point retractor (Balfour pediatric retractor; George Tiemann), applied to the lateral edges of the abdominal incision and sternum. This retractor is elevated using a vertical post attached to a surgical board under the animal.

Make incision (~0.5 cm) on the left lateral edge of the gastric fundus and insert gastric catheter with the tip resting in the antrum. Place a purse-string suture around the incision and tied. And, additional knot is made around the catheter and the free ends of this knot and purse-string suture are tied together. Finally surgical glue (Vetbond) is applied to the incision area.

In some experiments, a cuff electrode is placed on either the ventral or dorsal abdominal vagus nerve trunk. Suture a ground electrode lead to connective tissue on the greater curvature of the stomach.
Place planar electrodes on surface of stomach and duodenum using 8-0 silk suture. Suture a ground electrode lead to connective tissue on the greater curvature of the stomach
Administer abdominal lavage with antibiotic (cefalexin, 1% w/v) in saline (0.9%, 100 ml).

Test electrode impedances. Grapevine Neural Interface Processor and a Nano2 recording headstage (electrophysiological recording and stimulation, Ripple LLC, Grapevine Neural Interface Processor and a Nano2 r)
Close abdomen using absorbable suture for muscle (silk, 2-0) and monofilament (3-0) for the skin.
Turn the animal to dorsal side up.
Fix the head in place using stereotaxic frame and earbars.
Make dorsal midline incision on the head.
Tunnel under the skin between head and neck incision. Pull electrode connectors through to the head.
Suture gastric tube to neck and close neck incision.
Clear muscle tissue from dorsal skull.
Drill 4 to 8 holes in the skull and place bone screws. Leave enough space between head of screw and skull to provide grip for bone cement.
Layer bone cement onto skull to form a base.
Use bone cement to mount connectors. Surround connectors with cement.

Measure electrode impedances. Grapevine Neural Interface Processor and a Nano2 recording headstage (electrophysiological recording and stimulation, Ripple LLC, Grapevine Neural Interface Processor and a Nano2 r)
Extubate. Remove from isoflurane exposure.
Monitor animal for recovery from isoflurane.
Administer 10 days of oral amoxicillin and 72 hours of buprenorphine (s.c.).
Remove sutures 2 weeks after surgery.
Remove food from the home cage 3 h before anesthesia 03:00:00
Apply facemask to delivery inhalational isoflurane (1 to 2%).
Measure body weight
Shave abdomen and dorsal neck and head.
Intubate using a 3.0 or 3.5 endotracheal tube.
Place ferret supine on table. Connect EKG leads and place rectal probe for recording body temperature (keep at 36 to 40C).
Administer analgesic: 0.05mg/kg buprenorphine (s.c.).
Leave ~6 cm of the leads with head connector extended from the dorsal neck incision, wrapped in sterile gauze and taped to surrounding skin.
Make an incision in the midline abdominal muscle to expose the organs.

Record 10 min of baseline gastrointestinal myoelectric responses. Grapevine Neural Interface Processor and a Nano2 recording headstage (electrophysiological recording and stimulation, Ripple LLC, Grapevine Neural Interface Processor and a Nano2 r)
Sterilize skin for incision with chlorohexidine and 70% isopropyl alcohol.
Monitor vitals with pulse oximeter, EKG, rectal temperature probe. Animal prepped following sterile procedure.
Administer 20 ml of warm saline (s.c.) and repeat every two hours.

This is accomplished by caudal retraction of the stomach and retraction of the liver to the right side of the animal using saline moistened gauze. In this position, the esophagus and vagi become accessible.
creator: Charles C Horn
